sub_vertical: growth infrastructure automation for SaaS and PLG (Product-Led Growth) teams

signals: Extensive developer documentation and guides for 381+ tech stacks
Ready-to-use prompts for integration with IDEs and codebases
Clear technical architecture and integration details available
Transparent about product features, security, and integrations
Categorized guides by framework, backend, auth, payments, AI, deployment, comms, analytics, and UI

unique_findings: Skene's core innovation is the use of AI-powered prompts that interact directly with a developer's codebase and IDE to automate growth infrastructure (activation, retention, freemium gating) for 100+ modern tools. This is a shift from traditional PLG tools that rely on UI overlays or external dashboards.

The platform provides a massive, highly granular knowledge base (381+ guides) with ready-to-use prompts tailored for specific tech stacks and combinations (e.g., Astro + Firebase, Django + Stripe), suggesting a significant investment in mapping growth patterns to code-level interventions.

Skene positions 'growth' not as an external analytics or onboarding layer, but as first-class infrastructure—code that is versioned, owned, and prompt-driven within the product itself. This is a novel architectural stance, aiming to replace the 'black box' third-party scripts with transparent, developer-owned logic.

The system claims to derive 'signals directly from your codebase' and provide a 'context layer for your AI', hinting at deep code analysis or static/dynamic code instrumentation, which is technically challenging and not trivial to replicate.

Integration with AI coding tools like Cursor and Claude Code is emphasized, suggesting a convergent pattern with top AI developer tooling startups (e.g., Replit, Cursor, Sourcegraph Cody), but focused on growth automation rather than general code assistance.

The guides highlight gaps in popular frameworks (e.g., Astro, Django, Next.js) where activation/retention logic is missing, and Skene provides code-level patterns to fill those gaps—this signals a deep understanding of developer pain points and a modular, extensible architecture.

story_angles.headline: How Skene Turns Natural Language Prompts Into Growth Infrastructure—No Manual Coding Required
story_angles.summary: Skene leverages a unique blend of agentic architectures, RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), and knowledge graphs to let developers automate growth loops directly from their IDEs using natural language. This approach replaces traditional, piecemeal PLG stacks with a single, prompt-driven workflow, streamlining implementation and reducing engineering friction.
evidence: Build patterns: Natural-Language-to-Code, Agentic Architectures, RAG, Knowledge Graphs
Ready-to-use prompts for 381+ modern tools, instantly automating onboarding, activation, and retention

story_angles.headline: Skene’s Secret Weapon: Growth Patterns Sourced From 381+ Modern Tools
story_angles.summary: Instead of generic templates, Skene’s knowledge base encodes growth patterns for hundreds of real-world stacks—from Astro to FastAPI to Next.js. This proprietary data enables context-aware automation, letting developers copy prompts tailored to their exact tech stack and use case, and reducing the guesswork in PLG implementation.
evidence: Growth Infrastructure Knowledge Base with 381+ guides
Each guide includes tool-specific prompts and step-by-step flows

story_angles.headline: Why Skene Says: Stop Building Features, Start Shipping Growth—Right From Your IDE
story_angles.summary: While most developer tools focus on feature velocity, Skene’s bet is that true product-led growth comes from automating user journeys, not just adding features. By embedding growth automation into the developer workflow, Skene challenges the notion that growth is a post-launch concern and makes it a first-class citizen in the codebase.
evidence: Tagline: 'Stop building features. Start shipping growth.'
Growth loops are triggered and managed directly in the IDE, not a separate dashboard

story_angles.headline: Replace Your Legacy PLG Stack With a Single Prompt: Skene’s 10x Developer Efficiency Play
story_angles.summary: Skene claims to eliminate the need for multiple growth tools by letting developers implement onboarding, retention, and freemium gating with a single, context-aware prompt. This not only slashes integration time but also reduces maintenance overhead, making growth automation accessible to smaller teams.
evidence: Ready-to-use Skene prompts automate growth infrastructure across frameworks, databases, auth, and payments
No-code/low-code approach for developers—just copy a prompt into Cursor or Claude

anti_patterns.description: The product appears to be a thin layer on top of LLM APIs (e.g., Claude), primarily offering prompts and automation flows that could be replicated by direct API use. There is little evidence of proprietary infrastructure beyond orchestrating prompts.
evidence: References to 'ready-to-use Skene prompt you can paste into Cursor or Claude to automate growth infrastructure'

Tech Stack: LLMs: ['Claude'], Approach: rag

anti_patterns.description: There is no clear data advantage, proprietary model, or technical differentiation. The guides and prompts are easily replicable, and the competitive moat is described as 'medium' in the context.

anti_patterns.description: The product operates in a crowded space (growth automation, PLG tooling, prompt orchestration) without a clear unique angle or positioning. Many competitors offer similar prompt-based automation.
evidence: References to 381+ guides for common tech stacks (Astro, Django, Next.js, etc.)
No clear differentiation from other prompt orchestration tools

competitors.name: Pendo
competitors.similarity: Both provide product-led growth (PLG) infrastructure, onboarding, retention, and analytics for SaaS products.
competitors.how_different: Skene connects directly to the codebase and IDE, automating growth loops from source code rather than relying on UI overlays or manual tour creation. Pendo requires manual setup and is more UI-centric.

competitors.name: Appcues
competitors.similarity: Both automate onboarding, user activation, and retention through in-app experiences.
competitors.how_different: Appcues uses UI overlays and widgets that require ongoing maintenance and can break with deployments. Skene generates onboarding and lifecycle automation by analyzing the codebase, not the UI, and updates automatically with code changes.

competitors.name: Mixpanel
competitors.similarity: Both provide analytics and user journey tracking to optimize onboarding and retention.
competitors.how_different: Mixpanel requires manual event instrumentation and is focused on analytics dashboards. Skene auto-generates analytics and growth flows by reading the codebase and integrates directly into the developer workflow.

competitors.name: PostHog
competitors.similarity: Both offer product analytics, event tracking, and PLG features for modern SaaS teams.
competitors.how_different: PostHog is analytics-first and requires explicit event tracking. Skene is growth-loop-first, automating the creation and maintenance of growth flows as code, and integrates with IDEs and code repositories.

competitors.name: Userflow
competitors.similarity: Both help SaaS teams automate onboarding and drive feature adoption.
competitors.how_different: Userflow overlays UI elements and tours on top of the app. Skene operates at the codebase level, generating growth infrastructure as code and updating automatically with each deploy.

differentiation.primary: Skene is the only PLG platform that connects directly to your codebase and IDE, automating onboarding, retention, and growth loops as code rather than through UI overlays or external dashboards.
differentiation.technical: Skene analyzes the source code (via read-only repo access) to generate and update growth flows, integrates with developer tools (Cursor, Windsurf, v0, Bolt), and runs as an MCP/agent in the developer's environment. No code changes or API modifications are required, and everything updates automatically with code pushes.
differentiation.business: Outcome-based pricing (pay only when users complete onboarding), a free tier for small teams, and a focus on developer-first, solo-founder, and engineering-led teams who want to avoid maintaining a separate growth stack.
differentiation.positioning: Skene positions itself as a replacement for legacy PLG stacks and UI-based onboarding tools, emphasizing code ownership, developer experience, and seamless integration with the existing development workflow.

secret_sauce.core_advantage: Automated PLG infrastructure that reads and understands your codebase to generate, update, and run growth loops directly from source code and the IDE, not from the UI or external scripts.
secret_sauce.defensibility: Deep integration with code repositories and developer tools, automatic synchronization with code changes, and an agent-based architecture that minimizes manual maintenance. This approach requires significant technical investment and understanding of code parsing, developer workflows, and IDE integration.
evidence: “Skene reads your codebase and automatically generates onboarding, analytics, and lifecycle automation. When you push code, everything updates itself.”
“Growth should simply be code. Code that you own, version, and prompt - just like the rest of your product.”

“Setup takes less than 60 seconds. Simply connect your GitHub or GitLab repository (read-only access), and Skene automatically analyzes your codebase to generate PLG flows. No code changes or API modifications required.”
“The same Agent that understands your codebase can guide Cursor, Windsurf, v0, or Bolt to write loops consistently.”

competitive_analysis.moat_explanation: Skene’s moat is based on its technical integration with the codebase and developer tools, which is more defensible than UI overlays but less so than proprietary data or network effects. Competitors could theoretically build similar integrations, but Skene’s head start in developer-centric automation and agent-based architecture provides a moderate barrier to entry. The moat will strengthen if Skene builds a robust ecosystem or achieves deep adoption among engineering-led teams.

Adding AI Activation to FastAPI + LangChain
